gestures.name: Deictic
gestures.description: Pointing at a general object or location with hand or arm. If the target is a screen/board use PointScreen; if it is the audience use PointAudience.

gestures.name: PointScreen
gestures.description: Pointing at a specific part of the screen, board, or material with hand, arm, or a pointer.

gestures.name: PointAudience
gestures.description: Pointing at students or the audience with hand or arm (e.g., nominating a specific student).

gestures.name: Beat
gestures.description: Short, repeated hand movements in rhythm with speech for emphasis (beats). Rhythm/stress is the purpose, not meaning. Do not code small or incidental hand motion.

gestures.name: Iconic
gestures.description: Depicting the shape, size, or motion of a concrete object with the hands.

gestures.name: Metaphoric
gestures.description: Representing an abstract concept through space or form (e.g., splitting past/future to left/right).

gestures.name: Emblematic
gestures.description: A conventional symbolic gesture with a fixed cultural meaning (thumbs-up, OK sign, raised palm meaning 'stop').

gestures.name: OpenPalm
gestures.description: Presenting with an open palm (palm up/forward) without raising the arm above the shoulder. If directed at the audience, use OpenPalmAudience instead.

gestures.name: OpenPalmAudience
gestures.description: Showing an open palm toward the audience/students to offer the floor, invite, or present (e.g., 'please speak' with palm facing the audience).

gestures.name: RaiseHand
gestures.description: Raising the arm above shoulder height to draw attention, signal stop, or nominate. Judged by the arm being raised, regardless of palm direction.

gestures.name: Writing
gestures.description: Writing or drawing directly on a board, whiteboard, or tablet (board work).

gestures.name: LookAtScreen
gestures.description: Turning gaze, head, or upper body toward the screen or board to look at it (without pointing). If pointing, use PointScreen.

gestures.name: TurnBack
gestures.description: Turning one's back to the audience (typically while writing on the board or operating the screen).

gestures.name: Cohesive
gestures.description: Pointing again at a previously mentioned target or location to link the discourse.

gestures.name: Nodding
gestures.description: Nodding the head up and down to indicate agreement or affirmation.

gestures.name: HeadShake
gestures.description: Shaking the head side to side to indicate disagreement or negation.
